# Relocation Jobs — resume & portfolio write-up

Copy anything below into your CV, LinkedIn, or portfolio. Metrics are from project docs/tests only (no invented “hours saved”).

---

## 1. One-line positioning

**Relocation Jobs** ([kuchup.com](https://kuchup.com)) is a full-stack system I built to discover visa/relocation-friendly European software roles, scrape them from employer ATS boards into a shared Postgres catalog, track applications per user in a web panel, and prepare tailored LaTeX/PDF resumes via a Claude Desktop MCP pipeline.

---

## 2. Resume bullets (pick 5–8)

**Relocation Jobs** — Personal project · Python / Flask / Postgres / React / Playwright / AWS · [kuchup.com](https://kuchup.com)

- Built an end-to-end **visa/relocation job platform**: company discovery from relocate.me → multi-ATS scrape → shared Postgres catalog → multi-user tracking board → MCP-assisted tailored CV/PDF workflow.
- Designed a **multi-ATS ingestion engine** covering **25** ATS type choices (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Personio, Workable, Workday, and others) with static + Playwright XHR detection, cached API endpoints, relevance keyword filters, and **asyncio concurrency up to 16**.
- Modeled data as a **shared catalog + per-user overlay** (apply / reject / not-for-me / pin / looking-to-apply), merged at read time into a filterable, paginated board API (`GET /api/board`, default **25** companies/page).
- Shipped a **Claude Desktop MCP apply assistant** (~20 tools): master resume variants by slug, gated JD-mirror reframe pipeline, LaTeX validation, `tectonic` PDF render, cover letters, and panel company workspace with live PDF preview and board CV/PDF badges.
- Hardened the codebase from a **v1 monolith into a v2 domain architecture** (catalog / scrape / fetch / panel / positions / mcp / web) with SQL confined to `repo.py` layers and **~299** automated tests (README notes a **90%** coverage gate on business modules).
- Migrated persistence from **Neon to AWS EC2 Docker Postgres** after concurrent scrapes timed out, then **colocated** panel + Redis + Caddy + a **6-hour** Playwright fetch worker on one EC2 host behind Cloudflare.
- Diagnosed and fixed a board-load performance incident: **678** uncached Postgres round-trips per Germany page load; reduced Germany board latency from ~**89–103s** to ~**1.7s**.
- Operated production as a **solo full-stack owner**: deploy scripts, slim panel image (scrape off) vs worker image (Playwright on), TLS via Caddy, Cloudflare origin lockdown, and public docs that never leak credentials.

---

## 3. Full portfolio narrative

### The problem

Looking for backend/software roles in Europe that offer **visa or relocation sponsorship** is painful: company lists live on sites like relocate.me, each employer uses a different Applicant Tracking System (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Personio, Workday, …), openings appear and disappear quickly, and every serious application needs a tailored resume. Doing that by hand across countries does not scale.

I built **Relocation Jobs** to close that loop: discover companies → detect and scrape ATS boards → store a durable shared catalog → let users track what they’ve applied to → and prepare per-position tailored CVs with an LLM-assisted but human-gated pipeline. The live product is at **[kuchup.com](https://kuchup.com)**.

Supported focus countries include **Germany, Netherlands, UK, and Portugal**, with panel support for custom countries (e.g. Armenia, Ireland).

---

### Product overview

The system has five user-visible surfaces:

1. **Company discovery** — pull employer lists and careers URLs (CLI `build_companies`).
2. **Job scraping / fetch** — detect ATS, scrape openings (CLI + in-panel fetch + scheduled worker).
3. **Job board panel** — search, filter, paginate, and track applications across companies.
4. **Application data (`/apply`)** — profile, pipeline prompts, and master LaTeX resume variants per user.
5. **Company workspace** — per-company positions with tailored LaTeX, PDF preview, cover letters, and board badges when artifacts exist.

Under the hood, Claude Desktop talks to a local **MCP server** so chat can pull job context, save tailored `.tex`, validate structure, and mark applied — while private data stays in Postgres (nothing sensitive committed to git).

---

### Company discovery

Starting from **relocate.me** country pages, a discovery pipeline finds careers URLs via static HTTP (common nav/paths) and, when needed, Playwright CTA discovery. Results land in Postgres, sorted by city → company size (smallest first) → name. This seeds the catalog before any job scrape runs.

---

### Scraping & ATS detection engine

The scrape layer is a large part of the engineering work. On first contact with a company, the system resolves how to fetch jobs:

1. **Known overrides** for tricky employers (`KNOWN_ATS`)  
2. **Static HTML/URL detection**  
3. **Playwright with XHR interception** to catch ATS API calls  
4. **Generic Playwright DOM fallback** when there is no clean REST board  

Resolved `ats_type` and `ats_url` are **cached in Postgres**, so later runs call ATS APIs directly instead of re-detecting every time.

I implemented adapters and support for **25 labeled ATS types**, including Greenhouse (US/EU), Lever (US/EU), Ashby, Personio, Workable, Recruitee, SmartRecruiters, TeamTailor, Workday, HiBob, JOIN, Deel, bol.com, Job Shop, and others, plus company-specific and generic paths.

Fetch runs use **asyncio + httpx** with default/max concurrency **16**; Playwright detection runs in a thread pool. A keyword **relevance filter** keeps focus on backend/software-style roles (include/exclude rules). Re-scrapes **merge by job URL**, preserving fetch timestamps and user tracking; jobs that disappear from an ATS remain as catalog orphans and can reappear on the board when the user still has tracking for them.

Country and per-company fetches run from the panel (admin country fetch; per-company fetch on cards). A separate EC2 **fetch worker** runs country scrapes on a **6-hour** schedule (sequential countries, concurrency **4**) and skips a cycle if a fetch is already running.

---

### Data model: shared catalog + per-user overlay

Architecturally this is closer to a small multi-tenant job board than a single personal spreadsheet:

- **Shared catalog** — companies, matching jobs, country metadata (no `user_id` on the jobs themselves).  
- **Per-user overlay** — job tracking (applied, rejected, not-for-me, pinned, looking-to-apply), company-level applied flags, users, fetch runs.  
- **MCP artifacts** — master resumes by slug, application profile + pipeline prompts, tailored tex/PDF and cover-letter tex/PDF per position.

The main board is a **derived read model** built each request: load country catalog → load that user’s tracking → merge → bucket jobs → reinject orphans per business rules → filter/search → paginate by visible offset. Admin aggregate stats live on a separate admin API so the board response stays relatively light (`user_stats` chip only).

Layer discipline in the active v2 code: **SQL only in `*/repo.py`**, services/routes orchestrate without embedding queries — a deliberate move after growing past an early monolith.

---

### Web panel & frontend

The panel is a **Flask** app with JSON APIs and a hybrid UI:

- Vanilla JS for board load, mutations, filters, fetch UI (`relocation_jobs/static/js/`)  
- **React 19 + Vite** islands for pagination, company cards, CV badges, and related widgets (`frontend/` → `static/dist/board.js`)  
- Sessions, admin bootstrap, optional registration  
- Toolbar layout: pagination → search → sort/filters → company cards  

Locally the active spine is v2 on port **5051** with scrape enabled; production serves a slim panel image with scrape disabled (scraping delegated to the worker).

Board UX includes visa-related filters, hide-applied, fetch-problem views, pin / looking-to-apply, and badges showing whether MCP has prepared a CV or cover letter for a role. Company names link into the company workspace.

---

### Application assistant (MCP + LaTeX + company workspace)

This is the product differentiator beyond “another job scraper.”

I built a **stdio MCP server** for Claude Desktop that does **not** call the Claude API and does **not** auto-submit applications. Claude Desktop (subscription chat) owns reasoning; the app supplies tools and durable storage:

| Capability | Examples |
|------------|----------|
| Context | `get_job_context`, `list_application_queue`, profile / pipeline / master resume CRUD |
| Writing | `save_tailored_tex`, `save_cover_letter_tex` |
| Quality gates | `validate_tex` (structure + fact checks vs master) |
| Artifacts | `render_pdf` / cover-letter render via configurable LaTeX (`tectonic` by default) |
| Board sync | `mark_applied` |
| Catalog ops | `add_company`, `add_position`, `save_position_description`, `update_position`, country/ATS helpers |

The intended workflow is an **interactive, gated JD-mirror reframe**: run one pipeline phase per turn, get user approval, add **additive** bullets that mirror the job description onto the full master CV (do not strip responsibilities without permission), then save tailored LaTeX. Cover letters are freeform LaTeX. PDF compilation can happen on the panel to save chat tokens.

The **company workspace** (`/company/<country>/<slug>`) lists positions, shows CV vs cover-letter tabs, LaTeX source, PDF preview, download, and re-render — completing the loop from MCP write → human review → upload to ATS → mark applied.

---

### Architecture evolution (v1 → v2)

Early versions grew quickly (panel god file, scrape monolith, JSON-oriented paths). I then rebuilt the active spine under domain packages:

`catalog`, `positions`, `panel`, `fetch`, `scrape`, `companies`, `users`, `mcp`, `admin`, `web`, `shared`, `core`, `db`

v1 remains reference behavior for parity, not for new features. That refactor was about **maintainability and correctness** under real SE constraints: clear import graph, predictable places for SQL, testable services, and deleting dead shims rather than papering over them.

A knowledge-graph index of the repo (~4k nodes / ~20k edges) shows dense clusters around board UI, DB transactions, scrape/merge, MCP application tools, fetch UI, and Playwright detection — reflecting how the system actually coalesced.

---

### Production, infrastructure, and ops

Production target is a **single AWS EC2 host** (Frankfurt) colocating:

| Piece | Role |
|-------|------|
| Postgres (Docker) | Source of truth |
| Redis | Optional country-label cache (Postgres fallback) |
| Panel (Gunicorn) | Public app behind Caddy |
| Fetch worker | Playwright + scheduled country scrapes |
| Caddy | TLS (Let’s Encrypt) for kuchup.com / www; raw IP returns 404 |
| Cloudflare | DNS; optional orange-cloud + security-group lock to Cloudflare egress |

Deploy is scripted (`ec2_app_deploy.sh`): rsync, build panel + worker + Caddy, status and worker logs. Panel and worker use **different Docker images** — slim panel without Playwright (`PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=0`) vs a heavier worker image — so the public service stays small while scrapes still run.

A major ops decision was migrating **off Neon serverless Postgres** onto EC2 Docker Postgres after multi-worker scrapes hit remote timeouts (documented failure mid-country scrape). Colocating the panel with Postgres later cut remote round-trip pain that made the UI feel “stuck.” Docs and deploy assets are written for a **public repo**: placeholders only; real IPs/passwords stay in gitignored env files.

---

### Performance & reliability story

In July 2026 the board looked hung with local panel → remote EC2 Postgres. Germany was worst (~**90s**). Profiling showed the real bug was not “JD payloads too big” but an **N+1 round-trip bomb**: location-label helpers called `all_country_labels()` hundreds of times per request with no cache — **678** Postgres round-trips for **98** German companies, while the actual jobs SQL was under a second.

After the fix, Germany board latency measured ~**1.7s** (previously ~**89–103s**); smaller boards dropped to sub-second. Admin stats were split so heavy aggregates load asynchronously. Remaining roadmap includes a stronger board **read-model / cursor pagination** design, already written up as an internal proposal.

---

### Testing & quality bar

Active suite for the v2 spine: **pytest** with in-memory Postgres mocks (tests never hit live ATS or production DB). At write-up time, **`pytest tests --collect-only` reports 299 tests collected**; README still cites ~166 for the primary suite framing and a **90% coverage gate** on business modules. Scraping and business-rule behavior are covered across unit and integration-style tests for board merge, tracking, MCP, and web routes.

Engineering standards in-repo: no secrets in docs, ask before structural moves, prefer deleting dead code, keep modules ordered and SQL at the repo boundary.

---

### What this demonstrates (for humans reading a resume)

This is not a tutorial CRUD app. End-to-end ownership includes:

- **Product thinking** — visa/relocation vertical, application tracking, CV prep as part of the same system  
- **Distributed scraping systems** — detection, adapters, concurrency, merge semantics, scheduled workers  
- **Backend modeling** — shared catalog vs per-user state, idempotency keys, orphan reinjection, migrations  
- **Full-stack UI** — paginated boards, hybrid React + JS, workspace UX for PDFs  
- **LLM tooling integration** — MCP tools, human-gated pipelines, validation before render, private data in DB  
- **Ops** — EC2/Docker/Caddy/Cloudflare, image split for memory, deploy automation, cost-conscious single-host design  
- **Debugging under production constraints** — measuring RT counts, writing postmortems, fixing real latency  
- **Architecture maturity** — deliberate v2 redesign after a fast first build  

---

### Tech stack (concise)

| Layer | Technologies |
|-------|----------------|
| Backend | Python 3, Flask, Gunicorn, Pydantic, psycopg |
| Scrape | httpx, BeautifulSoup, Playwright/Chromium, asyncio |
| Data | Postgres, Redis (optional) |
| Apply assistant | MCP (stdio), LaTeX / tectonic |
| Frontend | React 19, Vite 6, vanilla JS |
| Ops | AWS EC2, Docker, Caddy, Cloudflare, shell deploy scripts |
| Tests | pytest, asyncio, coverage, respx |

---

## 4. LinkedIn / Featured project blurb (~100 words)

**Relocation Jobs (kuchup.com)** — I built a visa/relocation job platform for European software roles: discover employers from relocate.me, auto-detect and scrape **25** ATS types into a shared Postgres catalog, and track applications in a multi-user Flask + React panel. A Claude Desktop MCP server supplies ~20 tools for a gated JD-mirror resume pipeline (LaTeX → PDF, cover letters, company workspace with live preview). I refactored a fast v1 into a domain-layered v2 codebase, migrated Neon → EC2 Docker Postgres, and run production on one AWS host (panel, Redis, Caddy, 6-hour fetch worker, Cloudflare). Fixed a board-load N+1 that cut Germany page latency from ~90s to ~1.7s.

---

## 5. Headline / framing variants

Use whichever matches the role you’re applying for:

| Target role | Suggested title line |
|-------------|----------------------|
| Backend / platform | *Built a multi-ATS job ingestion platform with Postgres catalog, asyncio scrape workers, and production AWS ops* |
| Full-stack | *Designed and shipped kuchup.com — scrape → catalog → multi-user board → MCP resume workspace* |
| Platform / infra-leaning | *Owned Neon→EC2 migration, colocated Flask/Postgres/Redis/Caddy, and scheduled Playwright fetch workers* |
| AI / tooling | *Integrated Claude Desktop via MCP for a human-gated LaTeX CV pipeline with validation and PDF render* |
| Short skills line | *Python · Flask · Postgres · Playwright · React · MCP · AWS/Docker · asyncio scraping* |

---

If you want next steps, I can trim this into a one-page CV project block, rewrite it in third person, or map specific bullets onto your master resume’s language stack (Go / Java / fullstack variants).

- Modeled data as a **shared catalog + per-user overlay** (apply / reject / not-for-me / pin / looking-to-apply), merged at read time into a filterable, paginated board API (`GET /api/board`, default **25** companies/page).
- Shipped a **Claude Desktop MCP apply assistant** (~20 tools): master resume variants by slug, gated JD-mirror reframe pipeline, LaTeX validation, `tectonic` PDF render, cover letters, and panel company workspace with live PDF preview and board CV/PDF badges.
- Hardened the codebase from a **v1 monolith into a v2 domain architecture** (catalog / scrape / fetch / panel / positions / mcp / web) with SQL confined to `repo.py` layers and **~299** automated tests (README notes a **90%** coverage gate on business modules).
- Migrated persistence from **Neon to AWS EC2 Docker Postgres** after concurrent scrapes timed out, then **colocated** panel + Redis + Caddy + a **6-hour** Playwright fetch worker on one EC2 host behind Cloudflare.
- Diagnosed and fixed a board-load performance incident: **678** uncached Postgres round-trips per Germany page load; reduced Germany board latency from ~**89–103s** to ~**1.7s**.
- Operated production as a **solo full-stack owner**: deploy scripts, slim panel image (scrape off) vs worker image (Playwright on), TLS via Caddy, Cloudflare origin lockdown, and public docs that never leak credentials.

---

## 3. Full portfolio narrative

### The problem

Looking for backend/software roles in Europe that offer **visa or relocation sponsorship** is painful: company lists live on sites like relocate.me, each employer uses a different Applicant Tracking System (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Personio, Workday, …), openings appear and disappear quickly, and every serious application needs a tailored resume. Doing that by hand across countries does not scale.

I built **Relocation Jobs** to close that loop: discover companies → detect and scrape ATS boards → store a durable shared catalog → let users track what they’ve applied to → and prepare per-position tailored CVs with an LLM-assisted but human-gated pipeline. The live product is at **[kuchup.com](https://kuchup.com)**.

Supported focus countries include **Germany, Netherlands, UK, and Portugal**, with panel support for custom countries (e.g. Armenia, Ireland).

---

### Product overview

The system has five user-visible surfaces:

1. **Company discovery** — pull employer lists and careers URLs (CLI `build_companies`).
2. **Job scraping / fetch** — detect ATS, scrape openings (CLI + in-panel fetch + scheduled worker).
3. **Job board panel** — search, filter, paginate, and track applications across companies.
4. **Application data (`/apply`)** — profile, pipeline prompts, and master LaTeX resume variants per user.
5. **Company workspace** — per-company positions with tailored LaTeX, PDF preview, cover letters, and board badges when artifacts exist.

Under the hood, Claude Desktop talks to a local **MCP server** so chat can pull job context, save tailored `.tex`, validate structure, and mark applied — while private data stays in Postgres (nothing sensitive committed to git).

```text
relocate.me
    → build_companies (careers URL discovery)
    → Postgres catalog
    → scrape / fetch (ATS detection + job listings)
    → Flask panel (catalog + per-user tracking merge)
    → MCP apply assistant (tailored tex → PDF)
    → company workspace + mark applied
```

---

### Company discovery

Starting from **relocate.me** country pages, a discovery pipeline finds careers URLs via static HTTP (common nav/paths) and, when needed, Playwright CTA discovery. Results land in Postgres, sorted by city → company size (smallest first) → name. This seeds the catalog before any job scrape runs.

---

### Scraping & ATS detection engine

The scrape layer is a large part of the engineering work. On first contact with a company, the system resolves how to fetch jobs:

1. **Known overrides** for tricky employers (`KNOWN_ATS`)
2. **Static HTML/URL detection**
3. **Playwright with XHR interception** to catch ATS API calls
4. **Generic Playwright DOM fallback** when there is no clean REST board

Resolved `ats_type` and `ats_url` are **cached in Postgres**, so later runs call ATS APIs directly instead of re-detecting every time.

Adapters and support cover **25 labeled ATS types**, including Greenhouse (US/EU), Lever (US/EU), Ashby, Personio, Workable, Recruitee, SmartRecruiters, TeamTailor, Workday, HiBob, JOIN, Deel, bol.com, Job Shop, and others, plus company-specific and generic paths.

Fetch runs use **asyncio + httpx** with default/max concurrency **16**; Playwright detection runs in a thread pool. A keyword **relevance filter** keeps focus on backend/software-style roles (include/exclude rules). Re-scrapes **merge by job URL**, preserving fetch timestamps and user tracking; jobs that disappear from an ATS remain as catalog orphans and can reappear on the board when the user still has tracking for them.

Country and per-company fetches run from the panel (admin country fetch; per-company fetch on cards). A separate EC2 **fetch worker** runs country scrapes on a **6-hour** schedule (sequential countries, concurrency **4**) and skips a cycle if a fetch is already running.

---

### Data model: shared catalog + per-user overlay

Architecturally this is closer to a small multi-tenant job board than a single personal spreadsheet:

- **Shared catalog** — companies, matching jobs, country metadata (no `user_id` on the jobs themselves).
- **Per-user overlay** — job tracking (applied, rejected, not-for-me, pinned, looking-to-apply), company-level applied flags, users, fetch runs.
- **MCP artifacts** — master resumes by slug, application profile + pipeline prompts, tailored tex/PDF and cover-letter tex/PDF per position.

The main board is a **derived read model** built each request: load country catalog → load that user’s tracking → merge → bucket jobs → reinject orphans per business rules → filter/search → paginate by visible offset. Admin aggregate stats live on a separate admin API so the board response stays relatively light (`user_stats` chip only).

Layer discipline in the active v2 code: **SQL only in `*/repo.py`**, services/routes orchestrate without embedding queries — a deliberate move after growing past an early monolith.

**v2 domain layout:**

| Domain | Role |
|--------|------|
| `catalog/` | Companies & jobs repo |
| `scrape/` | ATS boards, merge, enrich, relevance |
| `fetch/` | In-process asyncio country/company fetch |
| `panel/` | Board flatten, filters, pagination, stats |
| `positions/` | Apply / reject / not-for-me |
| `mcp/` | Claude Desktop MCP: application prep, tex → PDF |
| `web/` | Flask server, routes, deps |
| `users/`, `companies/`, `admin/`, `core/`, `db/` | Auth-adjacent, CRUD, shared core, migrations |

---

### Web panel & frontend

The panel is a **Flask** app with JSON APIs and a hybrid UI:

- Vanilla JS for board load, mutations, filters, fetch UI (`relocation_jobs/static/js/`)
- **React 19 + Vite** islands for pagination, company cards, CV badges, and related widgets (`frontend/` → `static/dist/board.js`)
- Sessions, admin bootstrap, optional registration
- Toolbar layout: pagination → search → sort/filters → company cards

Locally the active spine is v2 on port **5051** with scrape enabled; production serves a slim panel image with scrape disabled (scraping delegated to the worker).

Board UX includes visa-related filters, hide-applied, fetch-problem views, pin / looking-to-apply, and badges showing whether MCP has prepared a CV or cover letter for a role. Company names link into the company workspace.

---

### Application assistant (MCP + LaTeX + company workspace)

This is the product differentiator beyond “another job scraper.”

I built a **stdio MCP server** for Claude Desktop that does **not** call the Claude API and does **not** auto-submit applications. Claude Desktop (subscription chat) owns reasoning; the app supplies tools and durable storage:

| Capability | Examples |
|------------|----------|
| Context | `get_job_context`, `list_application_queue`, profile / pipeline / master resume CRUD |
| Writing | `save_tailored_tex`, `save_cover_letter_tex` |
| Quality gates | `validate_tex` (structure + fact checks vs master) |
| Artifacts | `render_pdf` / cover-letter render via configurable LaTeX (`tectonic` by default) |
| Board sync | `mark_applied` |
| Catalog ops | `add_company`, `add_position`, `save_position_description`, `update_position`, country/ATS helpers |

The intended workflow is an **interactive, gated JD-mirror reframe**: run one pipeline phase per turn, get user approval, add **additive** bullets that mirror the job description onto the full master CV (do not strip responsibilities without permission), then save tailored LaTeX. Cover letters are freeform LaTeX. PDF compilation can happen on the panel to save chat tokens.

The **company workspace** (`/company/<country>/<slug>`) lists positions, shows CV vs cover-letter tabs, LaTeX source, PDF preview, download, and re-render — completing the loop from MCP write → human review → upload to ATS → mark applied.

---

### Architecture evolution (v1 → v2)

Early versions grew quickly (panel god file, scrape monolith, JSON-oriented paths). I then rebuilt the active spine under domain packages and kept v1 as reference behavior for parity, not for new features. That refactor was about **maintainability and correctness** under real SE constraints: clear import graph, predictable places for SQL, testable services, and deleting dead shims rather than papering over them.

Target import graph:

```text
web/routes → web/deps → */service → */repo → core/db → psycopg
fetch/pipeline → scrape/*, catalog/repo
panel/service → catalog, positions, users
mcp → catalog, positions, users
```

---

### Production, infrastructure, and ops

Production target is a **single AWS EC2 host** (Frankfurt) colocating:

| Piece | Role |
|-------|------|
| Postgres (Docker) | Source of truth |
| Redis | Optional country-label cache (Postgres fallback) |
| Panel (Gunicorn) | Public app behind Caddy |
| Fetch worker | Playwright + scheduled country scrapes |
| Caddy | TLS (Let’s Encrypt) for kuchup.com / www; raw IP returns 404 |
| Cloudflare | DNS; optional orange-cloud + security-group lock to Cloudflare egress |

Deploy is scripted (`ec2_app_deploy.sh`): rsync, build panel + worker + Caddy, status and worker logs. Panel and worker use **different Docker images** — slim panel without Playwright (`PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=0`) vs a heavier worker image — so the public service stays small while scrapes still run.

A major ops decision was migrating **off Neon serverless Postgres** onto EC2 Docker Postgres after multi-worker scrapes hit remote timeouts (documented failure mid-country scrape). Colocating the panel with Postgres later cut remote round-trip pain that made the UI feel “stuck.” Docs and deploy assets are written for a **public repo**: placeholders only; real IPs/passwords stay in gitignored env files.

---

### Performance & reliability story

In July 2026 the board looked hung with local panel → remote EC2 Postgres. Germany was worst (~**90s**). Profiling showed the real bug was not “JD payloads too big” but an **N+1 round-trip bomb**: location-label helpers called `all_country_labels()` hundreds of times per request with no cache — **678** Postgres round-trips for **98** German companies, while the actual jobs SQL was under a second.

After the fix, Germany board latency measured ~**1.7s** (previously ~**89–103s**); smaller boards dropped to sub-second. Admin stats were split so heavy aggregates load asynchronously. Remaining roadmap includes a stronger board **read-model / cursor pagination** design, already written up as an internal proposal.

---

### Testing & quality bar

Active suite for the v2 spine: **pytest** with in-memory Postgres mocks (tests never hit live ATS or production DB). At write-up time, **`pytest tests --collect-only` reports 299 tests collected**; README cites ~166 for an earlier suite framing and a **90% coverage gate** on business modules. Scraping and business-rule behavior are covered across unit and integration-style tests for board merge, tracking, MCP, and web routes.

Engineering standards in-repo: no secrets in docs, ask before structural moves, prefer deleting dead code, keep modules ordered and SQL at the repo boundary.

---

### What this demonstrates (for humans reading a resume)

This is not a tutorial CRUD app. End-to-end ownership includes:

- **Product thinking** — visa/relocation vertical, application tracking, CV prep as part of the same system
- **Distributed scraping systems** — detection, adapters, concurrency, merge semantics, scheduled workers
- **Backend modeling** — shared catalog vs per-user state, idempotency keys, orphan reinjection, migrations
- **Full-stack UI** — paginated boards, hybrid React + JS, workspace UX for PDFs
- **LLM tooling integration** — MCP tools, human-gated pipelines, validation before render, private data in DB
- **Ops** — EC2/Docker/Caddy/Cloudflare, image split for memory, deploy automation, cost-conscious single-host design
- **Debugging under production constraints** — measuring RT counts, writing postmortems, fixing real latency
- **Architecture maturity** — deliberate v2 redesign after a fast first build

---

### Tech stack (concise)

| Layer | Technologies |
|-------|----------------|
| Backend | Python 3, Flask, Gunicorn, Pydantic, psycopg |
| Scrape | httpx, BeautifulSoup, Playwright/Chromium, asyncio |
| Data | Postgres, Redis (optional) |
| Apply assistant | MCP (stdio), LaTeX / tectonic |
| Frontend | React 19, Vite 6, vanilla JS |
| Ops | AWS EC2, Docker, Caddy, Cloudflare, shell deploy scripts |
| Tests | pytest, asyncio, coverage, respx |

---

## 4. LinkedIn / Featured project blurb (~100 words)

**Relocation Jobs (kuchup.com)** — I built a visa/relocation job platform for European software roles: discover employers from relocate.me, auto-detect and scrape **25** ATS types into a shared Postgres catalog, and track applications in a multi-user Flask + React panel. A Claude Desktop MCP server supplies ~20 tools for a gated JD-mirror resume pipeline (LaTeX → PDF, cover letters, company workspace with live preview). I refactored a fast v1 into a domain-layered v2 codebase, migrated Neon → EC2 Docker Postgres, and run production on one AWS host (panel, Redis, Caddy, 6-hour fetch worker, Cloudflare). Fixed a board-load N+1 that cut Germany page latency from ~90s to ~1.7s.

---

## 5. Headline / framing variants

Use whichever matches the role you’re applying for:

| Target role | Suggested title line |
|-------------|----------------------|
| Backend / platform | *Built a multi-ATS job ingestion platform with Postgres catalog, asyncio scrape workers, and production AWS ops* |

| Platform / infra-leaning | *Owned Neon→EC2 migration, colocated Flask/Postgres/Redis/Caddy, and scheduled Playwright fetch workers* |
| AI / tooling | *Integrated Claude Desktop via MCP for a human-gated LaTeX CV pipeline with validation and PDF render* |
| Short skills line | *Python · Flask · Postgres · Playwright · React · MCP · AWS/Docker · asyncio scraping* |

# Projects master (reframe evidence store)

## What this is

A **separate MCP artifact type**: reusable **project descriptions** (markdown/prose), keyed by slug per user — same multi-variant pattern as master resumes (`go` / `java`), but **not** part of the LaTeX CV template and **not** validated as employers/years.

Agents use them during reframe to pick JD-aligned evidence (stack, metrics, architecture stories). Injecting a short Projects block onto a tailored CV remains an **additive, user-approved** step — same rules as mirror bullets.

Seed content for the first project can come from [`docs/portfolio/project-narrative.md`](docs/portfolio/project-narrative.md) (condensed CV-useful form), saved under slug e.g. `relocation-jobs`.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  applyUI["/apply Projects tab"] --> api["/api/mcp/project-masters"]
  mcpTools["list/get/save_project_master"] --> svc[mcp/service]
  api --> svc
  svc --> repo[mcp/repo]
  repo --> db["mcp_project_masters"]
  reframe["mcp-resume-reframe skill"] --> mcpTools
  reframe --> masterCV["get_master_resume"]
```

## Non-goals

- Do not auto-append projects into every tailored CV
- No PDF/render path for project masters (text/markdown only)
- Do not treat project names as employers in `validate_tex`
- Do not put full portfolio novels into tailored `.tex` by default — keep masters rich; tailored CV stays lean with user approval

## Data model

New migration `mcp_project_masters_v1` in [`relocation_jobs/core/migrations.py`](relocation_jobs/core/migrations.py):

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mcp_project_masters (
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  slug TEXT NOT NULL,
  label TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  content TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, slug)
);
```

Reuse the same slug normalizer as masters (`normalize_master_resume_slug` or rename to a shared `normalize_mcp_slug` in [`relocation_jobs/mcp/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/repo.py)).

**Types** in [`relocation_jobs/mcp/types.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/types.py): `ProjectMasterSummary` (`slug`, `label`, `updated_at`) — no `has_pdf`.

## Backend layers (clone the master-resume path, drop PDF)

| Layer | Changes |
|-------|---------|
| [`repo.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/repo.py) | `list_project_masters`, `get_project_master_row`, `read_project_master`, `save_project_master` |
| [`service.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/service.py) | Matching list/detail/save; extend `get_mcp_status` with `project_master_count` / slugs |
| [`server.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/server.py) | MCP tools: `list_project_masters`, `get_project_master(slug)`, `save_project_master(slug, content, label="")` |
| [`web/routes/mcp.py`](relocation_jobs/web/routes/mcp.py) | `GET/PUT /api/mcp/project-masters`, `GET /api/mcp/project-masters/<slug>` (login session) |
| [`mcp/__init__.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/__init__.py) | Export new service symbols if others are exported |

Content is freeform markdown (document that convention in docs + UI placeholder); no schema enforcement beyond non-empty slug.

## Panel (`/apply`)

Extend [`relocation_jobs/static/apply.html`](relocation_jobs/static/apply.html) + [`relocation_jobs/static/js/apply.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/apply.js):

- Third tab: **Projects** next to Profile | Master resumes
- Same UX as masters: list by slug/label, editor for slug/label/content, Save
- No Render PDF / PDF preview controls
- Placeholder text pointing at “full narrative for MCP reframe — not pasted wholesale into the CV”

## Reframe integration (skill + docs)

Update [`.claude/skills/mcp-resume-reframe/SKILL.md`](.claude/skills/mcp-resume-reframe/SKILL.md) and [`docs/reference/mcp-application.md`](docs/reference/mcp-application.md):

**Bootstrap (once per job):** after masters, also call `list_project_masters`; if JD-aligned, `get_project_master(slug)`.

**Phase 2:** recommend which project slug(s) supply evidence; keep “mirror experience” as a real employment role; projects are an **evidence bank**, not a fake employer.

**Phase 3–4:** with user approval only, may (a) prepend JD-aligned bullets on the mirror role using facts from the project master, and/or (b) add a short **Projects** LaTeX block if that master’s CV template already has / the user wants that section. Default remains: load projects for better truth; do not dump the full narrative into tailored tex.

**`get_mcp_status`:** surface project slugs so agents see what’s available.

## Tests

Mirror existing master tests in [`tests/mcp/test_mcp_service.py`](tests/mcp/test_mcp_service.py) / web MCP tests:

- save → list → get round-trip
- slug normalization / invalid slug errors

            FROM mcp_project_masters

def get_project_master_row(user_id: int, slug: str) -> dict | None:
    key = normalize_project_master_slug(slug)
    with db_read() as conn:
        row = conn.execute(
            """
            SELECT slug, label, content, updated_at
            FROM mcp_project_masters
            WHERE user_id = %s AND slug = %s
            """,
            (user_id, key),
        ).fetchone()
    return _row(row) if row else None


def read_project_master(user_id: int, slug: str) -> str:
    key = normalize_project_master_slug(slug)
    with db_read() as conn:
        row = conn.execute(
            """
            SELECT content
            FROM mcp_project_masters
            WHERE user_id = %s AND slug = %s
            """,
            (user_id, key),
        ).fetchone()
    if row is None or not (row.get("content") or "").strip():
        raise LookupError(f"Project master not found: {key}")
    return row["content"]


def save_project_master(
    user_id: int,
    slug: str,
    content: str,
    *,
    label: str = "",
) -> dict:
    key = normalize_project_master_slug(slug)
    now = _utc_now()
    with db_transaction() as conn:
        conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO mcp_project_masters (user_id, slug, label, content, updated_at)
            VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
            ON CONFLICT (user_id, slug) DO UPDATE SET
                label = EXCLUDED.label,
                content = EXCLUDED.content,
                updated_at = EXCLUDED.updated_at
            """,
            (user_id, key, (label or "").strip(), content, now),
        )
    return {"user_id": user_id, "slug": key, "label": (label or "").strip(), "updated_at": now}

input.contents:
# Relocation Jobs (kuchup.com)

Personal / solo project — Python, Flask, Postgres, React, Playwright, AWS.

## One-liner

Visa/relocation-focused European software job platform: discover companies from relocate.me → multi-ATS scrape → shared Postgres catalog → multi-user tracking panel → Claude Desktop MCP tailor-resume / cover-letter / PDF pipeline. Live at https://kuchup.com.

## Highlights (documented metrics only)

- End-to-end visa/relocation job platform: discovery → scrape → catalog → board → MCP CV/PDF workflow.
- Multi-ATS ingestion: **25** ATS type choices (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Personio, Workable, Workday, …); static + Playwright XHR detection; cached `ats_type`/`ats_url`; relevance filters; asyncio concurrency up to **16**.
- Shared catalog + per-user overlay (apply / reject / not-for-me / pin / looking-to-apply); paginated board API default **25** companies/page.
- Claude Desktop MCP apply assistant (~20+ tools): master resume variants, gated JD-mirror reframe, LaTeX validation, tectonic PDF, cover letters, company workspace with live PDF preview.
- v1 monolith → v2 domain packages (catalog / scrape / fetch / panel / positions / mcp / web); SQL only in `repo.py`; ~299 pytest tests; 90% coverage gate on business modules (README).
- Neon → AWS EC2 Docker Postgres; colocated panel + Redis + Caddy + **6-hour** Playwright fetch worker; Cloudflare + kuchup.com.
- Board N+1 fix: **678** uncached Postgres RTs / 98 German companies → Germany board ~**89–103s** down to ~**1.7s**.

## Architecture (agent cheat sheet)

```text
relocate.me → build_companies → Postgres catalog
  → scrape/fetch (ATS detect + listings)
  → Flask panel (catalog + per-user merge)
  → MCP (tailored tex/PDF) → company workspace → mark_applied
```

Domains: `catalog`, `scrape`, `fetch`, `panel`, `positions`, `mcp`, `web`, `users`, `companies`, `admin`, `core`, `db`.

## Stack

Python 3, Flask, Gunicorn, Pydantic, psycopg, httpx, BeautifulSoup, Playwright, Postgres, Redis (optional), MCP stdio, LaTeX/tectonic, React 19 + Vite, AWS EC2, Docker, Caddy, Cloudflare.

## Countries

Germany, Netherlands, UK, Portugal + custom (e.g. Armenia, Ireland).

## How agents should use this

            VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
            ON CONFLICT (user_id, slug) DO UPDATE SET
                label = EXCLUDED.label,
                content = EXCLUDED.content,
                updated_at = EXCLUDED.updated_at,
                pdf_bytes = CASE
                    WHEN mcp_project_masters.content IS DISTINCT FROM EXCLUDED.content THEN NULL
                    ELSE mcp_project_masters.pdf_bytes
                END,
                pdf_updated_at = CASE
                    WHEN mcp_project_masters.content IS DISTINCT FROM EXCLUDED.content THEN NULL
                    ELSE mcp_project_masters.pdf_updated_at
                END
            """,
            (user_id, key, (label or "").strip(), content, now),
        )
    return {"user_id": user_id, "slug": key, "label": (label or "").strip(), "updated_at": now}

\documentclass[11pt,a4paper]{article}
\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage{lmodern}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\usepackage{enumitem}
\usepackage{booktabs}

\hypersetup{colorlinks=true,linkcolor=blue!50!black,urlcolor=blue!60!black}

  basicstyle=\ttfamily\small,
  breaklines=true,

\title{Relocation Jobs (kuchup.com)\\
\large Comprehensive project reference}
\author{Solo / personal project}
\date{Last updated: 2026-07-08}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
\textbf{Relocation Jobs} (\url{https://kuchup.com}) is a full-stack system for discovering
visa- and relocation-friendly European software engineering roles, scraping
employer applicant tracking systems (ATS) into a shared Postgres catalog,
tracking applications per user in a web panel, and preparing tailored LaTeX/PDF
resumes and cover letters through a Claude Desktop Model Context Protocol (MCP)
pipeline. This document is the complete project master---an evidence and
architecture reference for agents and humans. It is \emph{not} a CV excerpt;
nothing here is meant to be pasted wholesale onto a resume without deliberate editing.
\end{abstract}

\tableofcontents
\newpage

\section{Positioning and product intent}

Looking for backend and software roles in Europe that offer visa or relocation
sponsorship is painful: company lists live on sites such as relocate.me; each
employer uses a different ATS (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Personio, Workday, and
many others); openings appear and disappear quickly; and every serious application
needs a tailored resume. Doing that by hand across countries does not scale.

Relocation Jobs closes that loop end to end:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item Discover companies and careers URLs.
  \item Detect and scrape ATS boards into a durable shared catalog.
  \item Let users track what they have applied to, rejected, pinned, or are
        looking to apply for.
  \item Prepare per-position tailored CVs (and optional cover letters) with an
        LLM-assisted but \textbf{human-gated} MCP pipeline.
\end{enumerate}

The live product is \textbf{\url{https://kuchup.com}}. Focus countries include
Germany, Netherlands, UK, and Portugal, with panel support for custom countries
(for example Armenia or Ireland).

\section{End-to-end data flow}

\begin{lstlisting}
relocate.me (country page)
    -> build_companies (careers URL discovery) -> Postgres catalog
    -> scrape / fetch (ATS detection + job listings) -> Postgres catalog
    -> Flask panel (catalog + per-user tracking merge)
    -> MCP apply assistant (tailored tex / PDF, cover letters)
    -> company workspace + mark_applied
\end{lstlisting}

Private application data (profile, masters, tailored artifacts) lives in Postgres
per user. Nothing sensitive is committed to the public git repository; docs use
placeholders such as \texttt{<ELASTIC\_IP>} rather than live credentials.

\section{User-visible surfaces}

\begin{enumerate}
  \item \textbf{Company discovery (CLI)} --- \texttt{build\_companies}: pull employer
        lists and careers URLs from relocate.me (and related discovery), write into
        Postgres. Sort order: city $\rightarrow$ company size (smallest first)
        $\rightarrow$ name.
  \item \textbf{Job scraping / fetch} --- CLI \texttt{scrape\_jobs}, in-panel country
        and per-company fetch, and a scheduled EC2 Playwright worker.
  \item \textbf{Job board panel} --- search, filter, paginate (default 25 companies
        per page), track applications; local v2 on port 5051 with scrape enabled;
        production panel scrape-disabled with worker handling scrapes.
  \item \textbf{Application data (\texttt{/apply})} --- profile, pipeline prompts
        (up to five ordered reframe phases), master resume LaTeX variants by slug
        (\texttt{go}, \texttt{java}, \texttt{fullstack}, \ldots), and
        \textbf{project masters} (this document's table / slug).
  \item \textbf{Company workspace} ---
        \texttt{/company/<country>/<company-slug>}: positions, tailored LaTeX,
        PDF preview, cover-letter tab, re-render; board badges (CV/PDF) when MCP
        has prepared artifacts.
\end{enumerate}

\section{Company discovery}

Starting from relocate.me country pages, discovery finds careers URLs via static
HTTP (common nav paths) and, when needed, Playwright CTA discovery. Results land
in the Postgres catalog and seed later scrapes. Single-company and sort-only
modes exist for incremental work.

\section{Scraping and ATS detection}

\subsection{Detection cascade (first contact)}

On first contact with a company, the system resolves how to fetch jobs:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item \textbf{Known overrides} --- \texttt{KNOWN\_ATS} for employers where
        auto-detection fails or returns a bad slug.
  \item \textbf{Static HTML/URL detection}.
  \item \textbf{Playwright with XHR interception} to catch ATS API calls.
  \item \textbf{Generic Playwright DOM fallback} when there is no clean REST board.
\end{enumerate}

Resolved \texttt{ats\_type} and \texttt{ats\_url} are \textbf{cached in Postgres} so
later runs call ATS APIs directly.

\subsection{ATS coverage}

There are \textbf{25} labeled ATS type choices in
\texttt{core/ats\_constants.ATS\_TYPE\_CHOICES}, including (non-exhaustive):
Ashby, Atlassian, ApplyToJob, BambooHR, bol.com API, Deel, EPAM, Greenhouse
(US/EU), HireHive, Jibe, Job Shop / Talents Connect, JOIN, Lever (US/EU),
movingimage, Personio, Project A, Recruitee, RSS, SmartRecruiters, Teamtailor,
Workable, Workday, HiBob---plus adapters and helpers under
\texttt{relocation\_jobs/scrape/boards/}.

\subsection{Concurrency, relevance, and merge semantics}

\begin{itemize}
  \item Fetch uses \textbf{asyncio + httpx} with default/max concurrency
        \textbf{16} (\texttt{MAX\_CONCURRENCY}); Playwright detection runs in a
        thread pool. Serial mode available via CLI.
  \item Keyword \textbf{relevance filters} (include/exclude) keep focus on
        backend/software-style roles.
  \item Re-scrapes \textbf{merge by job URL}, preserving fetch timestamps and
        user tracking. Jobs that disappear from an ATS remain catalog
        \emph{orphans} and can reappear on the board when the user still has
        tracking for them.
  \item Job descriptions can be enriched/stored for apply assist
        (\texttt{description\_text}); LinkedIn-style roles can be added manually
        via MCP \texttt{add\_position} / \texttt{save\_position\_description}.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Scheduled production fetch}

On EC2, container \texttt{relocation-fetch-worker} runs country scrapes every
\textbf{6 hours} (sequential countries, concurrency \textbf{4}). It skips a cycle
if another fetch is already running (\texttt{fetch\_runs.status = running}).

\section{Data model: shared catalog + per-user overlay}

This is closer to a small multi-tenant job board than a personal spreadsheet:

\begin{description}
  \item[Shared catalog] Companies, matching jobs, country metadata. Catalog jobs
        have no \texttt{user\_id}.
  \item[Per-user overlay] \texttt{job\_tracking} / company tracking: applied,
        rejected, not-for-me, pinned, looking-to-apply; users; fetch runs.
  \item[MCP artifacts] Master resumes by slug; application profile + pipeline;
        project masters (LaTeX evidence); per-application tailored tex/PDF and
        cover-letter tex/PDF.
\end{description}

Jobs are keyed for identity via idempotency (URL-normalized keys). Business rules
for buckets, orphans, and mutations live in
\texttt{docs/reference/business-rules.md}.

\subsection{Board as a derived read model}

Each \texttt{GET /api/board} request:

\begin{enumerate}
  \item Loads catalog for the selected country only.
  \item Loads that user's tracking scoped to the country.
  \item Merges tracking onto catalog jobs.
  \item Routes each job into buckets (\texttt{jobs}, \texttt{rejected\_jobs},
        \texttt{not\_for\_me\_jobs}).
  \item Reinjects orphans per business rules.
  \item Applies panel filters + search; paginates with visible offset
        (default page size 25, max 100).
\end{enumerate}

Full admin stats live on \texttt{GET /api/admin/panel-stats}; the board returns
lightweight \texttt{user\_stats} only.

\section{Web panel and frontend}

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Backend:} Flask v2 under \texttt{relocation\_jobs/web/} (routes for
        board, jobs, companies, fetch, catalog, admin, auth, mcp).
  \item \textbf{Client:} hybrid---vanilla JS for board load/mutations
        (\texttt{static/js/}) plus React~19 + Vite islands
        (\texttt{frontend/} $\rightarrow$ \texttt{static/dist/board.js}) for
        pagination, company cards, CV badges, widgets.
  \item Layout: pagination $\rightarrow$ search $\rightarrow$ sort/filters
        $\rightarrow$ company cards.
  \item Auth: Flask sessions, Werkzeug password hashes, admin bootstrap,
        optional registration.
\end{itemize}

\section{MCP application assistant}

\subsection{Design stance}

A local \textbf{stdio MCP server} (\texttt{scripts/mcp\_server.py} $\rightarrow$
\texttt{relocation\_jobs/mcp/}) for Claude Desktop:

\begin{itemize}
  \item Does \textbf{not} call the Claude API.
  \item Does \textbf{not} auto-submit applications to ATS forms.
  \item Claude Desktop (subscription chat) owns reasoning; the app owns data,
        validation, PDF compile (\texttt{tectonic} / configurable
        \texttt{MCP\_LATEX\_CMD}), and board updates.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Core tools (illustrative)}

Job context and queue; master resume CRUD; \textbf{project master} CRUD;
profile and reframe pipeline; \texttt{save\_tailored\_tex} /
\texttt{validate\_tex} / \texttt{render\_pdf}; cover letter save/render;
\texttt{mark\_applied}; catalog helpers (\texttt{add\_company},
\texttt{add\_position}, \texttt{save\_position\_description},
\texttt{update\_position}, country/ATS lists).

\subsection{JD-mirror reframe (interactive)}

Gated pipeline: one phase per turn with user checkpoints. Binding rules:

\begin{itemize}
  \item Start from the \textbf{full} chosen master CV.
  \item \textbf{Additive only}---do not delete/shorten master bullets without
        explicit permission.
  \item Mirror: pick one real employment role; add 1--2 JD-aligned bullets.
  \item Project masters are an \textbf{evidence bank}, not fake employers.
  \item Prefer panel Re-render PDF over chat \texttt{render\_pdf} to save tokens.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Project masters (this artifact)}

Table \texttt{mcp\_project\_masters}: \texttt{(user\_id, slug)} with
\texttt{label}, LaTeX \texttt{content}, optional cached PDF. Panel tab under
\texttt{/apply}. Used by agents via \texttt{list\_project\_masters} /
\texttt{get\_project\_master}. PDF preview wraps fragments in a minimal document
class when content has no \texttt{\textbackslash documentclass} of its own;
this file is a full compilable document.

\section{Architecture evolution (v1 $\rightarrow$ v2)}

Early growth: panel god file, scrape monolith, JSON-oriented paths. Active spine
is domain packages under \texttt{relocation\_jobs/}:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{@{}ll@{}}
\toprule
Domain & Role \\
\midrule
\texttt{catalog/} & Companies \& jobs repo \\
\texttt{scrape/} & ATS boards, merge, enrich, relevance \\
\texttt{fetch/} & In-process asyncio country/company fetch \\
\texttt{panel/} & Board flatten, filters, pagination, stats \\
\texttt{positions/} & Apply / reject / not-for-me \\
\texttt{mcp/} & Claude Desktop MCP: prep, tex $\rightarrow$ PDF \\
\texttt{web/} & Flask server, routes, deps \\
\texttt{users/}, \texttt{companies/}, \texttt{admin/} & Auth-adjacent, CRUD, aggregates \\
\texttt{core/}, \texttt{db/} & Shared core, migrations \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Layer rule: SQL only in \texttt{*/repo.py}. v1 remains reference-only for parity;
do not extend it. Target import graph: routes $\rightarrow$ deps $\rightarrow$
service $\rightarrow$ repo $\rightarrow$ \texttt{core/db} $\rightarrow$ psycopg.

\section{Production and operations}

Single AWS EC2 host (Frankfurt) colocating:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{@{}ll@{}}
\toprule
Piece & Role \\
\midrule
Postgres (Docker) & Source of truth \\
Redis & Optional country-label cache (Postgres fallback) \\
Panel (Gunicorn) & Public app behind Caddy; slim image, scrape off \\
Fetch worker & Playwright + 6h scheduled scrapes \\
Caddy & TLS for kuchup.com / www; raw Elastic IP returns 404 \\
Cloudflare & DNS; optional orange-cloud + SG lock to CF egress \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Deploy scripted via \texttt{scripts/ec2\_app\_deploy.sh}. Panel and worker use
different Docker images so the public service stays memory-light. Persistence
migrated from Neon serverless to EC2 Docker Postgres after multi-worker scrapes
hit remote timeouts. Colocating the panel with Postgres cut remote round-trip
latency that previously made the UI feel hung.

\section{Performance postmortem (board load)}

In July 2026 the local board (panel $\rightarrow$ remote EC2 Postgres) looked
frozen; Germany was worst ($\sim$90\,s). Root cause: N+1 round trips---location-label
helpers called \texttt{all\_country\_labels()} hundreds of times per request with

a written proposal.

\begin{itemize}

  \item Engineering standards: no secrets in public docs; ask before structural
        moves; prefer deleting dead code; ordered modules; SQL at the repo boundary.
\end{itemize}

\section{Tech stack}

\begin{description}

  \item[Scrape] httpx, BeautifulSoup, Playwright/Chromium, asyncio

  \item[Frontend] React~19, Vite~6, vanilla JS

\end{description}

\section{Documented metrics cheat sheet}

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{@{}lp{7.5cm}@{}}

Metric & Value / note \\

ATS type choices & 25 (\texttt{ATS\_TYPE\_CHOICES}) \\

\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Only documented numbers belong here; do not invent ``hours saved'' claims.

\section{What this project demonstrates}

\begin{itemize}
  \item Product thinking for a visa/relocation vertical spanning scrape, tracking,

  \item Multi-ATS ingestion: detection, adapters, concurrency, merge/orphan semantics,

# Relocation Jobs

Visa- and relocation-focused job search for backend and software roles in Europe.

Company lists come from [relocate.me](https://relocate.me). Each employer’s ATS is auto-detected; openings are scraped into a **shared Postgres catalog**. A multi-user Flask panel tracks applications. A **Claude Desktop MCP** pipeline prepares tailored LaTeX/PDF resumes and cover letters per position — with **project masters** as an evidence bank for reframing.

**Production:** [https://kuchup.com](https://kuchup.com) (AWS EC2 + Cloudflare)  
**Countries:** Germany, Netherlands, UK, Portugal, plus custom countries (e.g. Armenia, Ireland) via the panel.

> **Contributors / docs:** [`docs/contributing.md`](docs/contributing.md) · [`docs/README.md`](docs/README.md) · [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md)

---

## Features

| Area | What you get |
|------|----------------|
| **Company discovery** | Careers URL discovery from relocate.me → Postgres (`build_companies`) |
| **ATS ingestion** | 25 ATS type choices (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Personio, Workable, Workday, …) + Playwright fallback; cached `ats_type` / `ats_url` |
| **Concurrent scrape** | asyncio + httpx (up to **16** workers); relevance include/exclude keywords |
| **Shared catalog + per-user overlay** | Tracking (applied / reject / not-for-me / pin / looking-to-apply) merged at read time |
| **Web panel** | Paginated board (`GET /api/board`, default **25**/page), filters, fetch, add company |
| **Scheduled fetch (prod)** | EC2 Playwright worker every **6 hours** |
| **Application assistant** | Claude Desktop MCP: masters, project masters, gated JD-mirror reframe, validate, PDF (`tectonic`) |
| **Company workspace** | `/company/<country>/<slug>` — positions, CV / cover letter, live PDF preview, board CV/PDF badges |

---

## Quick start

```bash
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
python3 -m playwright install chromium

cp .env.example .env
# Set DATABASE_URL at minimum (local Postgres is fastest for dev)

PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=1 python3 scripts/panel_server.py
# → http://127.0.0.1:5051
```

On first startup the app creates the Postgres schema and bootstraps an admin from `PANEL_ADMIN_USER` / `PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD`. If no password is set, a random one is printed to the terminal.

After editing React UI: `cd frontend && npm run build` → `relocation_jobs/static/dist/board.js`. Hard refresh (`Cmd+Shift+R`) after JS/CSS changes.

---

## Environment

Copy `.env.example` → `.env`. Real hosts/passwords stay in gitignored `.env` / `aws-postgres.env` — this repo is **public**; docs use placeholders only.

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | **Required.** Postgres (local or AWS EC2) |
| `REDIS_URL` | Optional country-label cache (`scripts/ec2_redis.sh`); Postgres fallback when unset |
| `PANEL_SECRET_KEY` | Flask session signing |
| `PANEL_ADMIN_USER` / `PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD` | Bootstrap admin on first run |
| `PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED` | `1` locally for fetch; `0` on slim production panel image |
| `PANEL_DATA_DIR` | Local data dir for `custom_cities.json` (default `data/`) |
| `PANEL_ALLOW_REGISTER` | Self-service registration after first user |
| `MCP_USERNAME` / `MCP_USER_ID` | Panel user for Claude Desktop MCP (default `admin`) |
| `MCP_LATEX_CMD` | LaTeX compiler for PDF (default `tectonic`) |

MCP docs: [mcp-application.md](docs/reference/mcp-application.md) · [company-workspace.md](docs/reference/company-workspace.md).  
AWS Postgres: `./scripts/aws_postgres_migrate.sh sync-sg` after your public IP changes — [ops index](docs/README.md).

---

## Usage

### Web panel

```bash
PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=1 python3 scripts/panel_server.py   # → :5051
```

Sign in → select a **single country** → **Fetch** (admin) to scrape. Board: `GET /api/board` (pagination → search → sort/filters). Aggregate stats: admin page / `GET /api/admin/panel-stats` (board only returns lightweight `user_stats`).

Re-scrapes **merge by URL** — fetch dates and tracking are preserved. Jobs gone from an ATS stay as catalog orphans and reappear if you still have tracking.

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/` | Job board |
| `/apply` | Profile, pipeline prompts, **master resumes**, **project masters** (LaTeX + optional PDF) |
| `/company/<country>/<slug>` | Positions, tailored CV / cover letter, PDF preview, re-render |

**Claude Desktop MCP:** `python3 scripts/mcp_server.py` — job context, application queue, masters, project masters, tailored tex/PDF, cover letters, `mark_applied`, add company/position. See [mcp-application.md](docs/reference/mcp-application.md).

### Build company lists

```bash
python3 scripts/build_companies.py netherlands
python3 scripts/build_companies.py uk "Monzo"          # single company
python3 scripts/build_companies.py netherlands --sort-only
```

Sort: city (A–Z) → company size (smallest first) → name (A–Z).

### Scrape jobs

```bash
python3 scripts/scrape_jobs.py --country uk
python3 scripts/scrape_jobs.py --country uk "Monzo"
python3 scripts/scrape_jobs.py --country netherlands --skip-filled
python3 scripts/scrape_jobs.py --country germany --workers 16   # default 16
python3 scripts/scrape_jobs.py --country uk --serial
python3 scripts/scrape_jobs.py --all
```

First run per company: detect + cache ATS. Later runs hit the ATS API directly.

### Reset password

```bash
python3 scripts/reset_password.py <username>
```

---

## Architecture

```
relocate.me
    → build_companies          → Postgres catalog
    → scrape / v2 fetch        → Postgres catalog
    → web/ (Flask)             → board API (catalog + per-user merge)
    → mcp/                     → tailored tex / PDF / project masters
    → /company/…               → workspace + mark_applied
```

### Data stores

| Store | Contents |
|-------|----------|
| **Postgres** (`DATABASE_URL`) | Catalog, users, tracking, fetch runs, MCP artifacts (masters, projects, applications) |
| **Redis** (`REDIS_URL`) | Optional country-label cache |
| `companies/*.json` | Git archive only — not read at runtime |
| `data/custom_cities.json` | User-added cities (`PANEL_DATA_DIR`) |

### Package layout (`relocation_jobs/`)

```
catalog/      Postgres company + job reads/writes
positions/    Apply, reject, not-for-me, pin, looking-to-apply
panel/        Board flatten, pagination, filters, stats
fetch/        In-process asyncio country + company fetch
scrape/       ATS boards, merge, enrich, relevance
mcp/          Claude Desktop MCP — masters, projects, tex → PDF
web/          Flask server + routes
companies/    Company CRUD
users/        Users, applied history
admin/        Dashboard aggregates
core/         db, auth, ATS constants, detection
db/           Migrations bootstrap
static/       UI (+ dist/board.js from frontend/)
```

**Layer rule:** SQL only in `*/repo.py`. Details: [architecture.md](docs/reference/architecture.md) · [rules.md](docs/reference/rules.md).

### Board API

`GET /api/board` — `page`, `page_size` (default 25, max 100), `country`, `q`, filter flags. **Visible-offset** pagination after flatten + filters. “Newest first” is **client-side on the current page**; server returns catalog order. Full flow: [board.md](docs/reference/board.md).

### ATS detection (first run)

```
careers_url
  → 1. KNOWN_ATS override
  → 2. detect_ats_static()
  → 3. detect_ats_via_playwright()   # XHR intercept
  → 4. generic Playwright DOM fallback
```

### Job filtering

Titles must match an **include** keyword and no **exclude** keyword (`INCLUDE_KEYWORDS` / `EXCLUDE_KEYWORDS` in `core/ats_constants.py`; `scrape/relevance.py`).

---

## Testing

```bash
pytest tests -o addopts=                 # v2 suite (~299 collected)
pytest tests/mcp -o addopts=             # MCP / application assistant
pytest tests/test_route_manifest.py -o addopts=
pytest --cov --cov-report=term-missing   # 90% gate on business modules
```

In-memory Postgres mock only — no live ATS or production DB. Contracts: [business-rules.md](docs/reference/business-rules.md).

---

## Deployment (kuchup.com)

One AWS EC2 host: Postgres + Redis + panel + fetch worker + Caddy. Guide: [ec2-panel.md](docs/operations/ec2-panel.md).

| Service | Role |
|---------|------|
| Postgres (`pg`) | Source of truth |
| Redis | Country-label cache when configured |
| Panel (`relocation-panel`) | Gunicorn; slim image; `PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=0` |
| Fetch worker (`relocation-fetch-worker`) | Playwright; country scrape every **6h** (concurrency **4**) |
| Caddy | TLS + reverse proxy for kuchup.com / www (raw IP → 404) |
| Cloudflare | DNS; optional orange-cloud + origin lock-down |

```bash
./scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh deploy      # frontend build, rsync, images, restart
./scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh sync        # UI / static only
./scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh status
./scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh worker-logs
```

Requires gitignored `aws-postgres.env` and deploy SSH key. After laptop IP changes: `./scripts/aws_postgres_migrate.sh sync-sg`.

**Why colocated EC2:** board latency dropped far below Render → remote Postgres. Local scrape (`PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=1`) and the EC2 worker both write the same catalog.

`render.yaml` remains an optional legacy free-tier path (scrape off).

### Local Docker smoke test

```bash
docker build -t relocation-jobs .
docker run --rm -p 8080:10000 \
  -e PORT=10000 \
  -e PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD=changeme123 \
  -e DATABASE_URL="postgresql://..." \
  relocation-jobs
```

---

## Troubleshooting

**Domain doesn’t resolve locally** — try `dig @1.1.1.1 kuchup.com A`, flush DNS, or use `1.1.1.1` / `8.8.8.8`. Use `curl -I https://kuchup.com`, not `ping https://…`.

**Board empty on EC2 but API works** — rebuild React: `cd frontend && npm run build` then `./scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh sync`.

**Company returns 0 jobs** — clear cached ATS / re-scrape; check Network tab on careers page; add `KNOWN_ATS` in `core/ats_constants.py`.

**Playwright detection hangs** — ~25s timeout; pages behind login/cookie walls often need `KNOWN_ATS`.

**ATS 401/403** — try Greenhouse/Lever US vs EU variants; Recruitee needs the real slug.

**Wrong careers URL** — copy the live careers CTA URL; update in panel or re-run `build_companies.py` for that employer.

**Board feels hung (~minutes)** — often N+1 / remote Postgres; see [board-load postmortem](docs/reference/board-load-performance-incident.md). Prefer local Postgres for day-to-day UI work.

---

## Development docs

| Topic | Doc |
|-------|-----|
| First 15 minutes | [contributing.md](docs/contributing.md) |
| Architecture | [architecture.md](docs/reference/architecture.md) |
| Coding rules | [rules.md](docs/reference/rules.md) |
| Job buckets | [business-rules.md](docs/reference/business-rules.md) |
| MCP / apply / project masters | [mcp-application.md](docs/reference/mcp-application.md) |
| Agent commands | [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) |
| Full doc index | [docs/README.md](docs/README.md) |
